description: Mr. Nodeley apologizes — there was an existing wave 63 inventor-pairing mirror between BT4-092 and BT4-093 that Edgelord drew, sir, and the BT4-013 mom-print sat as a third anchor that the mirror could not reach without going lopsided. Three distinct registers (mom / inventor / explorer) on a single character across one set is the three-anchor cohort-establishment threshold the Tamiyo / Chandra / Trunks precedents established, so Mr. Nodeley begs the patience to commission the solo character node so the BT4-013 print can attach cleanly and any future Bulma prints from BT3 / Mythic Booster / Union Force can attach to the anchor rather than card-to-card mirroring. The Capsule-Corp time-machine engineering credit is the apparatus-of-engineering thesis-fit Alex flagged — civilian-engineering as load-bearing labor that warrior-arc protagonism depends on — and the node body anchors that reading via the labor hub cross-reference. Mr. Nodeley hopes this is useful and not too much trouble.
citations: [Dragon Ball manga chapter 1 (Akira Toriyama, Weekly Shonen Jump, 1984) — original character introduction + Dragon Radar invention]
[Dragon Ball Z manga, Trunks Saga (Toriyama, 1991) — canonical time-machine engineering credit]
[Dragon Ball Super Future Trunks Saga (Toei Animation, 2016) — present-timeline time-machine reverse-engineering]
[In-corpus BT4-013 Dependable Mom Bulma trivia — Wikipedia: Bulma citation chain]
node_body_highlights: Three canonical registers explicitly anchored: mom (BT4-013), inventor (BT4-092), explorer (BT4-093) — three distinct aspects across three distinct collector numbers within a single set, cohort-establishment threshold met

Capsule Corporation thesis section: Bulma engineered the Z-canon time machine that defines Future Trunks's existence — civilian-engineering as load-bearing labor that enables cosmic-scale warrior plotting (BBL labor / apparatus-of-engineering thesis fit)
Cross-character edges identified as future commissions: Trunks (son, strongest), Vegeta (husband), Son Goku (original adventure-companion from Dragon Ball ch1)
Macro-Cosmos / Orzhov-Syndicate comparison surfaced: Capsule Corporation as protagonist-side corporate-engineering apparatus, mirror to the antagonist-side corporate-power character nodes
Future-node candidates surfaced as parking-lot items: Dr. Brief (father, Capsule founder), Capsule Corporation faction node, Bulla (daughter)

frontmatter_wiring_required.action: add `characters: [bulma]` to frontmatter; the wave 63 Connections section card-to-card mirror to BT4-093 may be left intact as the inventor-dyad-within-set sub-edge (parallel to the Tamiyo aspect-pair pattern), or removed in a future Enlightened-Edgelord pass once the bulma node is the canonical anchor — parent's call
add `characters: [bulma]` to frontmatter; same wave-63-mirror note as BT4-092

wikilink_validity_audit.labor: expected at cards/_hubs/labor.md (BBL canonical hub per Alex memory)

other_candidates_considered: Saiyan race-collective node (10+ anchors available, parallel to namekian pattern) — deferred to future wave; Mr. Nodeley budget consumed by bulma this invocation
Piccolo / Bardock solo nodes — threshold-boundary; defer until anchor-count verified explicitly (Bardock relevant via BT4-117 Time-Trauma Masked Saiyan suspected-IP, flagged in E3 sidecar)
